report.title: Technical Remediation Report for Alvaro Obregon
report.summary: Alvaro Obregon is currently assessed at critical risk with score 83/100 based on 12 normalized reportable finding(s). This technical report preserves uncertainty, distinguishes evidence from inference, and orders remediation work by severity, evidence clarity, and verification readiness.
priorityActions: Priority 1: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 2: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 3: Enable HSTS after confirming HTTPS is stable for the site and subdomains. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 4: Add a tested Content-Security-Policy that limits script, frame, and object sources. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 5: Send X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.

report.title: Technical Remediation Report for Coyoacan
report.summary: Coyoacan is currently assessed at critical risk with score 83/100 based on 12 normalized reportable finding(s). This technical report preserves uncertainty, distinguishes evidence from inference, and orders remediation work by severity, evidence clarity, and verification readiness.
priorityActions: Priority 1: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 2: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 3: Enable HSTS after confirming HTTPS is stable for the site and subdomains. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 4: Add a tested Content-Security-Policy that limits script, frame, and object sources. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 5: Send X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.

technical.title: Technical Remediation Report for Coyoacan
technical.summary: Coyoacan is currently assessed at critical risk with score 83/100 based on 12 normalized reportable finding(s). This technical report preserves uncertainty, distinguishes evidence from inference, and orders remediation work by severity, evidence clarity, and verification readiness.
priorityActions: Priority 1: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 2: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 3: Enable HSTS after confirming HTTPS is stable for the site and subdomains. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 4: Add a tested Content-Security-Policy that limits script, frame, and object sources. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 5: Send X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.

friendly.title: Friendly Remediation Report for Coyoacan
friendly.summary: Coyoacan shows a critical level of risk with score 83/100 based on 12 main issue(s). This version explains the situation in simpler language, keeps the uncertainty visible, and highlights the most important next actions first.
priorityActions: Priority 1: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Why it matters: wordpress 6.4; confidence 0.8; references CVE-2024-31210.
Priority 2: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Why it matters: wordpress 6.4; confidence 0.8; references CVE-2024-31210.

Priority 3: Enable forced secure browsing rule after confirming HTTPS is stable for the site and subdomains. Why it matters: strict-transport-security was not present in the selected response headers.
Priority 4: Add a tested Content-Security-Policy that limits script, frame, and object sources. Why it matters: content-security-policy was not present in the selected response headers.

Priority 5: Send browser setting that blocks unsafe file-type guessing: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses. Why it matters: browser setting that blocks unsafe file-type guessing was not present in the selected response headers.

report.title: Technical Remediation Report for Tlalpan
report.summary: Tlalpan is currently assessed at critical risk with score 83/100 based on 12 normalized reportable finding(s). This technical report preserves uncertainty, distinguishes evidence from inference, and orders remediation work by severity, evidence clarity, and verification readiness.
priorityActions: Priority 1: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 2: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 3: Enable HSTS after confirming HTTPS is stable for the site and subdomains. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 4: Add a tested Content-Security-Policy that limits script, frame, and object sources. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 5: Send X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.

Priority 5: Send browser setting that blocks unsafe file-type guessing: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses. Why it matters: browser setting that blocks unsafe file-type guessing was not present in the selected response headers.

report.title: Technical Remediation Report for Saltillo
report.summary: Saltillo is currently assessed at critical risk with score 83/100 based on 12 normalized reportable finding(s). This technical report preserves uncertainty, distinguishes evidence from inference, and orders remediation work by severity, evidence clarity, and verification readiness.
priorityActions: Priority 1: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 2: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 3: Enable HSTS after confirming HTTPS is stable for the site and subdomains. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 4: Add a tested Content-Security-Policy that limits script, frame, and object sources. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 5: Send X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.

scope.title: What this report covers
scope.narrative: Saltillo was treated as the scoped public-facing target for this report. The report generator normalized the available structured input and limited the output to the provided target context.
bullets: Primary public URL: https://www.saltillo.gob.mx
Region or state reference: Coahuila

authorization.title: What was allowed
authorization.narrative: No explicit authorization object was supplied. The report generator treated the input as evidence-only and did not add new tests or claims.
bullets: No detailed authorization fields were supplied in the current input payload.

methodology.title: How this was reviewed
methodology.narrative: The report was assembled from structured inputs, normalized into a consistent evidence model, and then rendered into audience-specific fix guidance.
bullets: Reviewed structured scan evidence for https://www.saltillo.gob.mx.
Normalized flexible structured input into a consistent report contract before generating the PDFs.
Preserved only evidence-backed observations and did not add new findings without source support.
Excluded exploit payloads, credential use, and operational attack instructions from the output.

findingsOverview.title: What needs attention
findingsOverview.narrative: These are the main issues that deserve attention first. The list is ordered by likely impact and by how clear the available evidence is.
bullets: high: 2
medium: 8
low: 2
Top issue: WordPress 6.4 requires patch review
Top issue: WordPress 6.4 requires patch review
Top issue: Missing browser rule that forces secure connections

skippedTests.title: What was not tested
skippedTests.narrative: The current material does not cover every possible check, and the following activities were intentionally left out or not supplied.
bullets: Active exploitation, credential testing, fuzzing, destructive requests, and private-network actions were out of scope.

validationStatus.title: What was confirmed
validationStatus.narrative: These notes explain what the current evidence does and does not confirm.
bullets: The safe public target appeared reachable during the supplied observation window.
Observed HTTP status: 200.
No explicit controlled validation result was supplied in the structured input.

limitations.title: Important limits
limitations.narrative: These limits matter because they explain what still needs human follow-up before making a final decision.
bullets: Authorization scope details were not fully supplied.
No explicit validation result set was supplied, so unresolved uncertainty remains.
The report summarizes the provided evidence and should not be interpreted as proof of how easily the issue could be misused or breach.

remediationChecklist.title: Recommended next actions
remediationChecklist.narrative: These are the clearest next steps to reduce the risk in a practical way.
bullets: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release.
Assign an owner and due date before treating the item as resolved.
Confirm ownership for wordpress 6.4 requires patch review before the next review cycle.
Enable forced secure browsing rule after confirming HTTPS is stable for the site and subdomains.
Confirm ownership for missing browser rule that forces secure connections before the next review cycle.
Add a tested Content-Security-Policy that limits script, frame, and object sources.
Confirm ownership for missing browser rule that limits what the site can load before the next review cycle.
Send browser setting that blocks unsafe file-type guessing: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses.
Confirm ownership for missing browser setting that blocks unsafe file-type guessing before the next review cycle.
Restrict administrative entry points with access controls, MFA, and monitoring where operationally possible.

verificationGuidance.title: How to check the fixes
verificationGuidance.narrative: After each fix, use a simple follow-up review to confirm the public signs improved.
bullets: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.

convexPersistenceArgs.summary: Saltillo is currently assessed at critical risk with score 83/100 based on 12 normalized reportable finding(s). This technical report preserves uncertainty, distinguishes evidence from inference, and orders remediation work by severity, evidence clarity, and verification readiness.
priorityActions: Priority 1: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 2: Confirm the WordPress core and plugin patch level, then upgrade to the latest supported release. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 3: Enable HSTS after confirming HTTPS is stable for the site and subdomains. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 4: Add a tested Content-Security-Policy that limits script, frame, and object sources. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
Priority 5: Send X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff on HTML and static asset responses. Verification: Re-run the same bounded check after the mitigation is applied.
